packageRules.description: Exempt docker managers from minimumReleaseAge. Renovate's docker datasource only returns releaseTimestamp for Docker Hub (see lib/modules/datasource/docker/index.ts:86-88, 1078-1154); for GAR/gcr.io/ghcr.io/ECR/Quay it returns tags without timestamps. With minimumReleaseAge set and the default minimumReleaseAgeBehaviour=timestamp-required, these timestamp-less candidates are marked pendingChecks=true. When such a dep is grouped with a timestamped one (e.g. python-base grouped with the pep621 python interpreter via the python-runtime group), generate.ts:192 silently filters the pending one out, producing a single-dep PR. Setting 0 days here makes docker bumps ship without the quarantine, restoring grouped PRs. Side effect: external docker bumps (postgres, cloud-sdk) no longer require manual approval via the dashboard's Pending Status Checks list.

Disable automatic package-rename PRs. config:recommended pulls in replacements:all, whose community-maintained mappings open PRs proposing a deprecated package be swapped for its successor. A rename is exactly the kind of change we want a human to notice during a real build failure and adopt deliberately, not have routed through the autoreview automerge flow (the bot force-adds the autoreview label to every PR, so we can't keep these human-only by withholding the label). Disabling the replacement update type suppresses them entirely.
